name: HR Compliance Checklist
shortBenefit: Statutory HR compliance audit for Indian businesses.
intro: Audit your HR compliance status across key Indian labour laws including PF, ESIC, gratuity, minimum wage, maternity benefits and sexual harassment prevention.

seo.title: HR Compliance Checklist India — Labour Law Audit | Varada Nexus
seo.description: Free HR compliance checklist India. Audit PF, ESIC, gratuity, minimum wages, maternity benefits, POSH and other labour law compliance.

items.text: PF registration obtained (if 20+ employees)
PF returns filed monthly (ECR) by 15th
ESIC registration obtained (if 10+ employees in applicable states)
ESIC returns filed half-yearly
PF passbooks / UAN activated for all employees
Minimum wage paid as per state notification
Salary paid by 7th of following month (Shops & Establishments)
Gratuity Act compliance (if 10+ employees)
Payslips issued to all employees monthly
Maternity Benefit Act compliance (26 weeks leave)
Leave records maintained (EL, CL, SL) for all employees
Compensatory off / overtime records maintained
Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) constituted under POSH Act
POSH policy displayed and communicated to employees
Annual POSH report submitted to district officer
Workplace safety and health policy in place
Muster roll / attendance register maintained
Wages register maintained per Payment of Wages Act
Form 24Q / TDS on salary filed quarterly
Shops & Establishments registration current

assumptions: Requirements vary by employee count, state and industry. Consult a labour law practitioner for state-specific obligations.
disclaimer: This tool provides an indicative estimate for general planning only and is not professional, legal, financial, medical or engineering advice. Reference values are editable defaults that vary by location, vendor, regulation and date. Verify critical figures with a qualified professional. Varada Nexus accepts no liability for decisions made using this tool.

faq.a: PF registration is mandatory for establishments with 20 or more employees. Voluntary registration is possible for smaller establishments.
The Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) Act 2013 requires all organisations with 10+ employees to have an Internal Complaints Committee and a written anti-harassment policy.

faq.q: What are the penalties for non-compliance with labour laws?
faq.a: Penalties vary: PF default attracts damages of 5–25% plus interest; ESIC default attracts penalty up to twice the arrears. POSH non-compliance can attract fines up to ₹50,000.
